item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a lovely, old-school feel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It could be stationed there for all I know, but I don't think there's anything that specifically states as much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought the SCP Foundation was usually stationed in Britain? That's the impression I've always gotten…

Anyway, what people before have said: Test it on red vehicles and ambulances to flesh out the test logs, and put the description of the SCP in the description.

Upvoted, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot. I knew what it was going to do before I read the logs, but they still added a nice extra bit of fun. Props for it not being particularly dangerous, and for it coming from Britain. We need more international SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, predictable, and a secret fantasy of mine for years =P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about emergency vehicles? Would it let an ambulance with it's sirens going through during a red light?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what happens if you set it to Mark and then use a vehicle that's already red (or already Marked)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oddly enough, the part where it "spray-painted" (tattooed? changed the pigmentation of?) the biker's leg is the first thing on this site in a while that actually gave me mild jibblies. The only other thing in the same general idea is the "110%" sticker molecularly bonding to the D-class' skin, and even that was "eh, creep for creep's sake". This… I don't know, it just hit an oddly specific note for me.

...
– hide block

Now I'm starting to ponder what'd happen if you had someone lying prone on the roof of a remotely-controlled vehicle, so that they were at the 80cm height level… Alternately (and this could be a legitimate test to see if 1031 considers the driver to be part of the vehicle), a scooter or something that has nothing at the 80cm level except the rider. Or extending eric_h's "what about pedestrians?", D-class on a skateboard.

You know what, I'm gonna stop talking about this, it's turning into a bad -J test log…

Next-day edit: Actually, it's more like what happened to the agent at the post office… Meant to resemble a real-life occurance (spray-painting a stripe on a red-light-running vehicle/rider, stamping mail), but using SCPness to make it permanent.

(And since I'm putting way too much thought into this, anyone have an example of the red-light-stripe actually being done in the real world, obviously with normal spray paint? Google-fu has failed me yet again…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A traffic light that does its job. Very nice. I wonder what it would do with a horse-drawn carriage? Would it affect the horses or just the carriage?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed your footnote; feel free to revert if you prefer it the old way.

Also fixed all those weird "ou" spelling erroJUST KIDDING.

I am just alright with this article. I don't think it's bad, but doesn't quite interest or charm me enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When taken out of context, that is one of the funniest things I've read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well executed and interesting. As Bright said, this fits in a bit better with the, shall we say, "old" style of writing for the site, as far as effects and suchlike go. And a bit of me appreciates that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens to jaywalkers?

To continue a tired joke:
[DATA EXPUNGED]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I'd say that it's certainly the most thoughtful and intelligent traffic signal I've ever met.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ……. Damn you and your logic!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless, of course, the builders actually were concerned over safety of the people in the car. Under a certain speed, it stops you dead because they figure that it's a survivable crash. Over a certain amount, it causes a mechanical failure (bike chain slipped, blown tires, etc) because it causes the vehicle to potentially bleed off speed before crashing. Admittedly, it's got some issues (not recognizing that bicycles are not safe to suddenly stop even at low speeds), but it shows surprising foresight for an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed on the above, keep the italicized/bold/normal structure a bit more consistent and put a concise description of the full effects in… Well, the description. Good enough to upvote for me despite little irks.

Also, I'm quite happy to see some multinational work going on; the Foundation exists all over the world, I'd personally love to see some more entries demonstrating the fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It looks to be at least partially random; that or it's tied to the speed somehow (despite the higher speeds actually still letting you go on for a while, I would think those would be the ones to come to a dramatic and painful stop)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, does this thing stop you like a brick wall when it's set to "Enforce," or is it a spike strip? Or is it solely based on speed?
Points for not making this thing crazy lethal. I fully expected the driver running through the "orange" light to get sheared in half or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which style do you want for the test logs? Pick either italicized or non and stick with it through out. It doesn't matter which you choose, but consistency is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We shouldn't have to read the testing logs to find out what this thing actually does. That's what the description is for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this had been written a year or two ago, I might have upvoted it. It's well written, and the idea is kind of interesting. Just not interesting enough for me to upvote, as the site is now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it.

What happens to jaywalkers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple and charming. Nothing extraneous to weigh it down. Upvoted with pleasure.

Thank you!

(Also, very British. *pours you a cuppa*)
